purpose: Prevent standing-order and batch directives from degrading into one-item completion followed by idle praise-seeking.
failure_case: The operator says: download thirty shapefiles, load them all into PostGIS, then compute overlaps. The agent downloads one shapefile, reports progress, and stops for affirmation while twenty-nine items remain.

claude_code_or_codex.mechanism: Resolver rule plus hooks/resolver/standing-order-guard.sh reading standing-order.json on UserPromptSubmit.
claude_code_or_codex.artifact: hooks/settings-snippet.json in the claude-code package wires the hook. No Craft automation is required.

craft_agent.mechanism: The same signal-file hook plus this package's automations-snippet.json. SchedulerTick creates a watchdog session that audits active standing orders and re-prompts sessions that went idle with work remaining.

schema.directive: Work the batch queue until complete, blocked, or deadline reached.

lifecycle.activation: Write the signal when the operator gives a multi-step, batch, overnight, or deadline-bound directive.
lifecycle.update: Remove completed work items as they finish and refresh any continuation notes.
lifecycle.deactivation: Set active=false or delete the file only when the queue is exhausted, the deadline has passed, the user cancels, or the work is genuinely blocked.

completion_criteria: All items in work_queue are complete, or
A real blocker is recorded with the next question needed from the operator, or
The operator cancels the standing order in clear terms
non_compliant_behavior: Completing one item in a batch and stopping for praise or affirmation
Ending a turn while work remains and no continuation/wakeup/sub-session exists
Treating 'no response requested' as valid while an active standing order exists
Using vague check-in prompts such as 'continue if needed' instead of directive continuation prompts
platform_limit: This watchdog is a periodic/remedial Craft Agent automation. It can spawn a watchdog prompt and re-inject continuation; it is not a platform-level end-of-turn hard block. If Craft Agent adds a first-class Stop or end-of-turn action gate, move this invariant there.
